# three-flatland

> 2D sprites, tilemaps, and effects for Three.js — TSL shaders, WebGPU, automatic batching.

**Status**: Early alpha. Install with `@alpha` tag.
**Repository**: https://github.com/thejustinwalsh/three-flatland
**Docs**: https://thejustinwalsh.com/three-flatland/

## Requirements

- three.js >= 0.183.1 (TSL/WebGPU support)
- koota >= 0.6.5 (peer, for ECS-driven batching)
- React >= 19.0.0 (for `three-flatland/react`)
- @react-three/fiber >= 10.0.0-alpha.2 (for React, WebGPU support)

## Installation

```bash
npm install three-flatland@alpha                              # Three.js
npm install three-flatland@alpha @react-three/fiber@alpha     # React Three Fiber
npm install @three-flatland/nodes@alpha                       # TSL effect nodes (optional)
npm install @three-flatland/skia@alpha                        # Skia vector graphics (optional)
```

## Imports

Three.js: `import { ... } from 'three-flatland'`
React: `import { ... } from 'three-flatland/react'` (re-exports core + ThreeElements JSX types)
Nodes: `import { ... } from '@three-flatland/nodes'` or per-category `'@three-flatland/nodes/color'`
Skia: `import { Skia } from '@three-flatland/skia'`, scene nodes from `'@three-flatland/skia/three'`, R3F from `'@three-flatland/skia/react'`
Tweakpane (examples only): `import { createPane, wireSceneStats } from '@three-flatland/devtools'`, React hooks from `'@three-flatland/devtools/react'`

Always use `WebGPURenderer` from `'three/webgpu'`, never `WebGLRenderer`. R3F users import from `'@react-three/fiber/webgpu'`, not `'@react-three/fiber'`.

---

## Sprite2D

2D sprite mesh. Extends `THREE.Mesh`.

```typescript
const sprite = new Sprite2D({
  texture,                         // Texture
  frame,                           // SpriteFrame (UV rect from spritesheet)
  anchor: [0.5, 0.5],             // Pivot point 0-1
  tint: 0xffffff,                  // Color | string | number
  alpha: 1,                        // Opacity 0-1
  flipX: false,
  flipY: false,
  layer: Layers.ENTITIES,          // Render layer (0-6)
  zIndex: 0,                       // Sort within layer
  pixelPerfect: false,
})
```

Properties: `texture`, `frame`, `anchor`, `tint`, `alpha`, `flipX`, `flipY`, `layer`, `zIndex`, `pixelPerfect`, `width` (read-only), `height` (read-only).
Methods: `setFrame()`, `setAnchor(x, y)`, `flip(h, v)`, `addEffect(effect)`, `removeEffect(effect)`, `clone()`, `dispose()`.

## AnimatedSprite2D

Extends `Sprite2D` with spritesheet animation.

```typescript
const player = new AnimatedSprite2D({
  spriteSheet: sheet,              // SpriteSheet from SpriteSheetLoader
  animationSet: {
    animations: {
      idle: { frames: ['idle_0', 'idle_1', 'idle_2'], fps: 8 },
      run: { frames: ['run_0', 'run_1', 'run_2', 'run_3'], fps: 12 },
      attack: { frames: ['attack_0', 'attack_1'], fps: 15, loop: false },
    },
  },
  animation: 'idle',               // Initial animation
})

player.update(deltaMs)              // Call each frame
player.play('attack', {
  onComplete: () => player.play('idle'),
  onEvent: (event, frame) => { /* per-frame events */ },
})
```

Methods: `play(name, options?)`, `pause()`, `resume()`, `stop()`, `gotoFrame(i)`, `update(deltaMs)`, `isPlaying()`.

## Flatland

Unified 2D rendering pipeline. Extends `THREE.Group`. Manages batching, camera, post-processing.

```typescript
const flatland = new Flatland({
  viewSize: 400,                   // Camera frustum half-height
  clearColor: 0x1a1a2e,
  aspect: window.innerWidth / window.innerHeight,
  postProcessing: false,           // Enable for PassEffects
})

flatland.add(sprite)               // Sprites route to internal SpriteGroup
flatland.render(renderer)          // Call each frame

window.addEventListener('resize', () => {
  renderer.setSize(window.innerWidth, window.innerHeight)
  flatland.resize(window.innerWidth, window.innerHeight)
})
```

Properties: `camera`, `viewSize`, `clearColor`, `autoClear`, `stats`.
Methods: `add()`, `remove()`, `render(renderer)`, `resize(w, h)`, `addPass(passEffect)`, `removePass(passEffect)`, `dispose()`.

## SpriteGroup

Automatic batching container. Sprites sharing a material batch into single draw calls.

```typescript
const group = new SpriteGroup()
scene.add(group)
group.add(sprite1)
group.add(sprite2)  // Same texture = 1 draw call
```

## Loaders

```typescript
// Textures — 'pixel-art' preset by default (NearestFilter, no mipmaps)
const texture = await TextureLoader.load('/sprite.png')
const smooth = await TextureLoader.load('/ui.png', { texture: 'smooth' })

// Spritesheets — TexturePacker JSON Hash or Array format
const sheet = await SpriteSheetLoader.load('/sprites/player.json')
sheet.getFrame('idle_0')           // SpriteFrame
sheet.getFrameNames()              // string[]

// R3F — both loaders work with useLoader()
const texture = useLoader(TextureLoader, '/sprite.png')
```

## Layers

Render order is decoupled from the scene graph. Use layers for category ordering, zIndex for sorting within a layer.

```typescript
import { Layers } from 'three-flatland'

Layers.BACKGROUND  // 0
Layers.GROUND      // 1
Layers.SHADOWS     // 2
Layers.ENTITIES    // 3
Layers.EFFECTS     // 4
Layers.FOREGROUND  // 5
Layers.UI          // 6

sprite.layer = Layers.ENTITIES
sprite.zIndex = -sprite.position.y  // Y-sort: lower sprites render on top
```

---

## Effects

There are two effect systems:

### MaterialEffect — per-sprite shader variations

Uses GPU instance attributes. Each sprite can have different parameter values while sharing one draw call.

```typescript
import { createMaterialEffect } from 'three-flatland'
import { mix, vec4 } from 'three/tsl'

const DissolveEffect = createMaterialEffect({
  name: 'dissolve',
  schema: { progress: 0 } as const,
  node({ inputColor, attrs }) {
    return mix(inputColor, vec4(0, 0, 0, 0), attrs.progress)
  },
})

const dissolve = new DissolveEffect()
sprite.addEffect(dissolve)
dissolve.progress = 0.5  // Per-sprite value
```

The `node` function receives `{ inputColor, inputUV, attrs }` where attrs contains TSL nodes for each schema field.

### PassEffect — full-screen post-processing

Uses TSL uniforms. Zero-cost parameter updates (no shader rebuild).

```typescript
import { createPassEffect } from 'three-flatland'
import { crtComplete } from '@three-flatland/nodes'

const CRTEffect = createPassEffect({
  name: 'crt',
  schema: { curvature: 0.1, scanlineIntensity: 0.2 } as const,
  pass: ({ uniforms }) => (input, uv) =>
    crtComplete(input, uv, {
      curvature: uniforms.curvature,
      scanlineIntensity: uniforms.scanlineIntensity,
    }),
})

const crt = new CRTEffect()
flatland.addPass(crt)
crt.curvature = 0.2  // Updates uniform directly
```

The `pass` function receives `{ uniforms }` and returns `(input, uv) => color`.

---

## TSL Effect Nodes

Import from `@three-flatland/nodes` or per-category subpaths. All functions accept and return TSL nodes.

**sprite** (`@three-flatland/nodes/sprite`): `uvScale`, `uvOffset`, `uvRotate`, `uvFlip`, `pixelate`, `pixelateBySize`, `outline`, `outline8`, `sampleSprite`, `spriteUV`

**color** (`@three-flatland/nodes/color`): `tint`, `tintAdditive`, `hueShift`, `hueShiftNormalized`, `saturate`, `grayscale`, `brightness`, `brightnessMultiply`, `brightnessClamped`, `contrast`, `contrastSCurve`, `colorRemap`, `colorRemapCustom`

**alpha** (`@three-flatland/nodes/alpha`): `alphaTest`, `alphaTestOpaque`, `alphaMask`, `alphaMaskValue`, `alphaMaskInvert`, `fadeEdge`, `fadeEdgeRadial`, `fadeEdgeHorizontal`, `fadeEdgeVertical`, `dissolve`, `dissolveSimple`, `dissolvePixelated`, `dissolveDirectional`

**retro** (`@three-flatland/nodes/retro`): `quantize`, `quantizeRGB`, `posterize`, `posterizeGamma`, `bayerDither`, `bayerDither2x2`, `bayerDither4x4`, `bayerDither8x8`, `colorReplace`, `colorReplaceHard`, `colorReplaceMultiple`, `palettize`, `palettizeDithered`, `palettizeNearest` (plus `consolePalettes` constants)

**distortion** (`@three-flatland/nodes/distortion`): `waveHorizontal`, `waveVertical`, `waveRadial`, `waveRipple`, `waveWater`, `distortPinch`, `distortBulge`, `distortTwirl`, `distortBarrel`, `distortPixelate`, `distortPixelateCount`, `distortShatter`, `distortDisplace`, `distortSpherize`, `distortNoise`, `distortPerlin`, `distortSimplex`, `distortTurbulence`

**vfx** (`@three-flatland/nodes/vfx`): `sparkle`, `sparkleStar`, `sparkleRainbow`, `shimmer`, `shimmerWave`, `shimmerHolographic`, `shimmerMetallic`, `pulse`, `pulseGlow`, `pulseHeartbeat`, `pulseRadial`, `pulseRainbow`, `flash`, `flashSmooth`, `flashAdditive`, `flashAlpha`, `flashDamage`, `flashHeal`, `flashPowerUp`, `trail`, `trailAdditive`, `trailBlur`, `trailVelocity`, `afterimage`, `afterimageTinted`, `afterimageGlow`, `ghost`, `ghostTrail`, `ghostSpeed`

**blur** (`@three-flatland/nodes/blur`): `blurGaussian`, `blurGaussian9`, `blurGaussian2Pass`, `blurBox`, `blurBoxDirectional`, `blurBox3x3`, `blurBoxSmooth`, `blurRadial`, `blurRadialDistance`, `blurSpin`, `blurFocus`, `blurMotion`, `blurMotionVelocity`, `blurMotionObject`, `blurShake`, `speedLines`, `blurKawase`, `blurKawaseSimple`, `blurKawaseDown`, `blurKawaseUp`, `blurKawaseMulti`, `bloom`, `bloomThreshold`, `bloomSimple`, `bloomAnamorphic`, `glowSelective`, `vignette`, `filmGrain`

**display** (`@three-flatland/nodes/display`): `scanlines`, `scanlinesSmooth`, `scanlinesGlow`, `scanlinesInterlaced`, `scanlinesGrid`, `phosphorApertureGrille`, `phosphorSlotMask`, `phosphorShadowMask`, `phosphorSimple`, `phosphorMask`, `crtCurvature`, `crtCurvatureWithCorners`, `crtVignette`, `crtBloom`, `crtColorBleed`, `crtComplete`, `crtConvergence`, `lcdGrid`, `dotMatrix`, `lcdGhosting`, `lcdMotionGhost`, `lcdBacklightBleed`, `lcdPocket`, `lcdGBC`

**analog** (`@three-flatland/nodes/analog`): `chromaticAberration`, `chromaticAberrationRadial`, `chromaticAberrationCustom`, `chromaticAberrationPulse`, `colorBleeding`, `interlacing`, `ntscComposite`, `vhsDistortion`, `staticNoise`, `analogGlitch`, `signalInterference`

**upscale** (`@three-flatland/nodes/upscale`): `scale2x`, `scale3x`, `eagle`, `superEagle`, `sai2x`, `hq2x`, `hq3x`, `hq4x`

All node functions follow the pattern: `fn(color_or_input, uv?, options?)` returning a TSL `Node<'vec4'>` (or `Node<'vec2'>` for UV transforms).

---

## Tilemaps

```typescript
import { TiledLoader } from 'three-flatland'  // or LDtkLoader

const tilemap = await TiledLoader.load('/maps/level1.json')
scene.add(tilemap)
tilemap.update(deltaMs)  // For animated tiles
```

Exports: `TileMap2D`, `Tileset`, `TileLayer`, `TiledLoader`, `LDtkLoader`. Types: `TileMapData`, `TileLayerData`, `ObjectLayerData`, `TileMapObject`, `TileDefinition`, `TileAnimationFrame`, `CollisionShape`, `ChunkCoord`, `TileInstance`.

---

## Skia (`@three-flatland/skia`)

GPU-accelerated 2D vector graphics via Skia WASM. Auto-detects WebGPU or WebGL from renderer. ~857 KB (WebGPU) / ~1 MB (WebGL) brotli vs CanvasKit's 2.2 MB. Optional separate package.

**Subpaths:**
- `@three-flatland/skia` — core: `Skia`, `SkiaContext`, `SkiaPaint`, `SkiaPath`, `SkiaFont`, `SkiaTypeface`, `SkiaImage`, `SkiaImageFilter`, `SkiaColorFilter`, `SkiaPathEffect`, `SkiaShader`, `SkiaPathMeasure`, `SkiaTextBlob`, `SkiaPicture`, `SkiaPictureRecorder`, `SkiaDrawingContext`. Types: `StrokeCap`, `StrokeJoin`, `BlendMode`, `BlurStyle`, `PathOp`, `TileMode`, `Path1DStyle`, `SkiaBackend`.
- `@three-flatland/skia/three` — Three.js scene nodes: `SkiaCanvas`, `SkiaNode`, `SkiaGroup`, `SkiaRect`, `SkiaCircle`, `SkiaOval`, `SkiaLine`, `SkiaPathNode`, `SkiaTextNode`, `SkiaImageNode`, `SkiaTextPathNode`, `SkiaFontLoader`, `SkiaTypeface`, `SkiaImageLoader`, `getFBOId`.
- `@three-flatland/skia/react` — R3F integration: `SkiaCanvas`, `useSkiaContext`, `attachSkiaTexture`, plus all scene nodes.

Drawing nodes (`SkiaRect`, `SkiaCircle`, `SkiaLine`, `SkiaPathNode`, `SkiaTextNode`, `SkiaImageNode`, `SkiaGroup`) use no-arg construction + property assignment (R3F-compatible). Paint props (`fill`, `stroke`, `strokeWidth`, `strokeCap`, `strokeJoin`, `opacity`, `blur`, `dash`, `blendMode`, `antiAlias`) live on every node — assign via setter or pass an explicit `paint` ref. `SkiaRect` props: `x`, `y`, `width`, `height`, `cornerRadius`. `SkiaCircle` props: `cx`, `cy`, `r`.

```typescript
// Three.js — render Skia shapes into a Three.js scene
import { Skia } from '@three-flatland/skia'
import { SkiaCanvas, SkiaRect, SkiaCircle } from '@three-flatland/skia/three'

await Skia.init(renderer)                          // auto-detects WebGPU/WebGL
const canvas = new SkiaCanvas({ renderer, width: 512, height: 512 })
await canvas.ready

const rect = new SkiaRect()
rect.x = 10; rect.y = 10; rect.width = 200; rect.height = 100
rect.fill = [1, 0, 0, 1]                           // SkiaColor: [r, g, b, a]
canvas.add(rect)

const circle = new SkiaCircle()
circle.cx = 256; circle.cy = 256; circle.r = 80
circle.fill = [0, 0.5, 1, 1]
canvas.add(circle)

canvas.invalidate()                                // mark dirty; re-records on next render
canvas.render(renderer)                            // call each frame (or only when invalidated)
```

```tsx
// R3F — declarative SkiaCanvas, child nodes set props via reconciler
import { SkiaCanvas, SkiaRect, SkiaTextNode } from '@three-flatland/skia/react'
import { SkiaFontLoader } from '@three-flatland/skia/three'
import { useLoader } from '@react-three/fiber/webgpu'

function MyOverlay() {
  const typeface = useLoader(SkiaFontLoader, '/fonts/Inter.ttf')
  return (
    <SkiaCanvas width={512} height={512} overlay>
      <SkiaRect x={10} y={10} width={200} height={100} fill={[1, 0, 0, 1]} />
      <SkiaTextNode text="Hello" font={typeface.atSize(48)} x={20} y={80} />
    </SkiaCanvas>
  )
}
```

For production, copy WASM with `npx skia-wasm public/skia` then pass `Skia.init(renderer, { wasmUrl: '/skia/skia-gl.wasm' })`. Vite dev mode is zero-config.

---

## Tweakpane helpers (`@three-flatland/devtools`)

Examples use this workspace package (NOT part of the library API — optional dev dependency) for debug controls and performance stats. Wraps Tweakpane v4 with a Flatland theme, an auto-wired renderer stats row, FPS/MS graph, and GPU timestamp query support.

**Core** (`@three-flatland/devtools`): `createPane(options)`, `wireSceneStats(scene, stats, options?)`, `addStatsGraph`, `applyTheme`, `FLATLAND_THEME`, `registerPlugins`, `EssentialsPlugin`. Types: `CreatePaneOptions`, `PaneBundle`, `StatsHandle`, `StatsUpdate`, `StatsGraphHandle`.

**React** (`@three-flatland/devtools/react`): `usePane()`, `usePaneFolder(parent, title)`, `usePaneInput(parent, key, initial, options?)`, `usePaneButton(parent, title, onClick)`, `useFpsGraph(parent)`, `useStatsMonitor(stats)`. Types: `PaneParent`, `PaneInputOptions`, `FpsGraphHandle`, `CreatePaneOptions`, `PaneBundle`, `StatsHandle`.

```typescript
// Three.js — createPane({ scene }) returns { pane, stats }
// stats row + FPS graph auto-wired; wireSceneStats hooks scene.onAfterRender
// to capture renderer.info.render + memory and drain GPU timestamp pool.
import { createPane } from '@three-flatland/devtools'
const { pane, stats } = createPane({ scene })
// pane.addFolder(...) — standard Tweakpane API
// stats.begin() / stats.end() — frame timing; auto-populated if you pass scene
```

```tsx
// R3F — hooks version. useStatsMonitor wires stats into the R3F frame loop
// (priority: Infinity for begin, -Infinity for end) + calls wireSceneStats.
import { usePane, usePaneFolder, usePaneInput, useStatsMonitor } from '@three-flatland/devtools/react'

function Controls() {
  const { pane, stats } = usePane()
  const folder = usePaneFolder(pane, 'Settings')
  const [speed] = usePaneInput(folder, 'speed', 1.0, { min: 0, max: 5, step: 0.1 })
  useStatsMonitor(stats)
  return null
}
```

Requires `trackTimestamp: true` on `WebGPURenderer` for GPU frame time. WebGL backend auto-detects `EXT_disjoint_timer_query_webgl2` and falls back to CPU timing when unavailable.

---

## Showcases

- [Breakout](https://thejustinwalsh.com/three-flatland/showcases/breakout/) — complete arcade game built on three-flatland + Koota ECS. StackBlitz embed + per-file source viewer.

---

## Canonical Examples

### Three.js — minimal sprite

```typescript
import { WebGPURenderer } from 'three/webgpu'
import { Scene, OrthographicCamera, Color } from 'three'
import { Sprite2D, TextureLoader, Layers } from 'three-flatland'

const scene = new Scene()
scene.background = new Color(0x1a1a2e)
const camera = new OrthographicCamera(-400, 400, 300, -300, 0.1, 1000)
camera.position.z = 100

const renderer = new WebGPURenderer({ antialias: true })
renderer.setSize(800, 600)
document.body.appendChild(renderer.domElement)
await renderer.init()

const texture = await TextureLoader.load('/sprite.png')
const sprite = new Sprite2D({ texture, anchor: [0.5, 0.5] })
sprite.layer = Layers.ENTITIES
scene.add(sprite)

function animate() {
  requestAnimationFrame(animate)
  renderer.render(scene, camera)
}
animate()
```

### React Three Fiber — minimal sprite

```tsx
import { Canvas, extend, useLoader } from '@react-three/fiber/webgpu'
import { Suspense } from 'react'
import { Sprite2D, TextureLoader } from 'three-flatland/react'

extend({ Sprite2D })

function MySprite() {
  const texture = useLoader(TextureLoader, '/sprite.png')
  return <sprite2D texture={texture} anchor={[0.5, 0.5]} />
}

export default function App() {
  return (
    <Canvas orthographic camera={{ zoom: 1, position: [0, 0, 100] }}>
      <color attach="background" args={['#1a1a2e']} />
      <Suspense><MySprite /></Suspense>
    </Canvas>
  )
}
```

### R3F — registering components

```tsx
import { extend } from '@react-three/fiber/webgpu'
import { Sprite2D, AnimatedSprite2D, SpriteGroup, Flatland } from 'three-flatland/react'

extend({ Sprite2D, AnimatedSprite2D, SpriteGroup, Flatland })
// JSX: <sprite2D>, <animatedSprite2D>, <spriteGroup>, <flatland>
```
